source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - South Marsh Island Block 223

event.top_event: Hand Crush Injury During Crane-Assisted Load Positioning
actions_taken: PDC Roustabout evacuated to Gulf Regional Occupational Medical Center (GROMC) in Abbeville, Louisiana
BSEE on-site investigation conducted on 23 March 2020
G-110 Incident of Noncompliance (INC) issued to Cox Operating, L.L.C.
event.summary: On 21 March 2020 at approximately 12:00 hours, a Parker Drilling Company Roustabout sustained a laceration and fracture to his right-hand middle finger while assisting in repositioning a three feet by six feet basket containing a 13.625-inch double studded adapter (DSA) on the keyway deck of the PDC 77B barge rig. The injury occurred when the DSA shifted inside the basket and rolled over the Roustabout's right hand, which was holding the top rim of the basket. The incident was caused by improper hand placement, inadequate job briefing, poor communication, and failure to follow the approved work procedure which required use of an air hoist.
key_phrases: pre-job safety meeting did not cover task

threats.name: Inadequate Job Safety Analysis and Pre-Job Briefing
Failure to Follow Approved Work Procedure
Improper Hand Placement During Load Handling
Load Not Properly Secured During Transfer

consequences.name: Hand Crush Injury - Laceration and Fracture

controls.name: Pre-Job Safety Meeting and Work Safe Procedure (WSP) Review
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure crew awareness of hazards and procedures

supporting_text: The PDC crew engaged in a pre-job safety meeting during which a Work Safe Procedure (WSP) and Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was reviewed.
The pre-JSA did not cover landing the basket on the keyway deck.
The JSA did not cover positioning the basket on the lower deck or using an air hoist.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for Basket Positioning Task
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and safe work methods for specific tasks

supporting_text: The task of moving a three feet by six feet basket that contained a 13.625-inch double studded adapter (DSA) from the pipe rack onto the keyway deck. This lift was more complicated because it involved placing the basket in tight spot on the keyway deck. This task was not discussed during the safety meeting.
The JSA did not cover positioning the basket on the lower deck or using an air hoist.

controls.name: Approved Work Procedure - Use of Air Hoist for Basket Landing
controls.barrier_role: Engineering and administrative control to ensure safe load positioning using mechanical assistance

supporting_text: The approved procedure for landing the basket on the keyway deck involved the use of an air hoist.
The PDC Crane Operator thought the rest of the PDC crew was using an air hoist to position the basket. The PDC Motorman and PDC Roustabout did not know using the air hoist was part of the procedure.
The PDC crew did not use an air hoist while landing the basket in the keyway.

controls.name: Crew Communication and Coordination During Load Handling
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure all crew members understand their roles and coordinate safely

supporting_text: Poor communications between all parties involved in the transfer operations

controls.name: Load Securing and Restraint During Transfer Operations
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent load shifting and movement during handling

supporting_text: The load in the basket was not secured properly during transfer operations.
When the basket swung back in and held by the PDC Motorman and PDC Roustabout, the DSA shifted inside the basket.

controls.name: Proper Hand Placement and Body Positioning Training
controls.barrier_role: Administrative and training control to prevent hand injuries during load handling

supporting_text: Improper hand placement by the PDC Roustabout
The PDC Roustabout held on the top rim of the basket when the DSA shifted.

controls.name: Operator Oversight and Safe Work Practices Enforcement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operations are performed in a safe and workmanlike manner

supporting_text: Cox Operating, L.L.C., (Cox) failed to oversee that operations were performed in a safe and workmanlike manner on the Parker Drilling Company (PDC) 77B barge rig during well operations

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
